title: Red Hat Security Advisory: kernel-rt security update
overview:
An update for kernel-rt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: kernel-rt security update

An update for kernel-rt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for kernel-rt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
kernel-rt-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.src.rpm
kernel-rt-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-core-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-core-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-debuginfo-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-devel-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-modules-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-modules-extra-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debuginfo-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debuginfo-common-x86_64-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-devel-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-modules-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-modules-extra-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-kvm-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-kvm-4.18.0-553.85.1.rt7.426.el8_10.x86_64.rpm

CVE-2025-39697: A flaw use after free in the Linux kernel NFS functionality was found in the way connected user sends malicious data to the server. A remote user could use this flaw to crash the system.

CVE-2025-39971: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

i40e: fix idx validation in config queues msg

Ensure idx is within range of active/initialized TCs when iterating over
vf->ch[idx] in i40e_vc_config_queues_msg().

CVE-2025-39973: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

i40e: add validation for ring_len param

The `ring_len` parameter provided by the virtual function (VF)
is assigned directly to the hardware memory context (HMC) without
any validation.

To address this, introduce an upper boundary check for both Tx and Rx
queue lengths. The maximum number of descriptors supported by the
hardware is 8k-32.
Additionally, enforce alignment constraints: Tx rings must be a multiple
of 8, and Rx rings must be a multiple of 32.
